findings.description: Full card number (PAN) and CVV are written to the application log in plaintext, a serious PCI-DSS violation (CVV must never be stored/logged).

Transaction IDs are derived from the non-cryptographic, per-process-salted built-in hash(), making them predictable, collision-prone, and non-deterministic across runs despite the 'deterministic' comment.
monthly_revenue_report computes revenue from raw line items without applying the per-line discount or tax that charge_order used, so reported revenue diverges from amounts actually charged.
Monetary values use float throughout (unit_price, subtotal, tax), risking floating-point rounding errors in money math where Decimal should be used.
compute_order_total applies tax without rounding the final total, so float residue flows into amount_cents at int(round(total*100)) and can yield off-by-one-cent charges.
charge_order reads order status then updates it without locking/idempotency (TOCTOU), allowing concurrent or retried calls to double-charge the same order.
Rounding each discounted line before summing can diverge from rounding the order total once, accumulating cent-level discrepancies.
A zero-item or zero-total order computes amount_cents==0 and surfaces gateway's 'non-positive amount' failure instead of explicitly handling empty orders.
discount_pct is never validated, so negative or >100 values silently produce inflated or negative line totals.
No error handling around the gateway.charge call; an SDK exception propagates uncommitted with no explicit rollback (relying on connection close to discard).
monthly_revenue_report issues an N+1 query, running one order_items SELECT per order inside the loop instead of a single JOIN/GROUP BY aggregate.
order["discount_pct"] or 0.0 coerces a stored 0 (or other falsy) to default, masking intent and any distinction between NULL and zero discount.
The cvv parameter is accepted by charge() but never used in the gateway logic (dead parameter besides being logged).
Mixed typing style: Optional from typing alongside PEP 585 builtin generics (list[LineItem]); prefer one convention.
